loopScoreRubric.principle: XP-lite and Definition of Done are harness-level design gates: every Ultrawork loop should prove a small focused change, relevant verification, live TUI observation, and clean next action before claiming progress.
definitionOfDone: No broad refactor mixed into the task.
Public behavior is covered by tests unless the change is purely cosmetic or documentation-only.
User-facing TUI/CLI surface changes are directly opened and observed before finishing.
xpLite: Inspect relevant files, tests, and project conventions first.
Write or update tests before implementation when practical for bugs and core logic.
Run the most relevant tests, typecheck, lint, and build commands available.
Fix verification failures or report blockers clearly.
Finish with changed files, behavior changed, tests run, and remaining risks.

references.name: Terminal-Bench
references.principle: terminal-native tasks should be evaluated by executable command traces and artifacts

references.name: TerminalWorld
references.principle: terminal-agent evaluations should move toward realistic tasks derived from human terminal workflows

references.name: SWE-bench Verified / holdout
references.principle: agent benchmarks need verified tasks, held-out coverage, and programmatic pass/fail checks

references.name: OSWorld
references.principle: live UI agents require inspectable environment evidence instead of claimed interaction

references.name: tau-bench
references.principle: tool-using agents should be judged on disciplined tool calls and task outcomes

references.name: VeRO
references.principle: agent harnesses need versioned snapshots, structured traces, and budget-controlled evaluation

references.name: Meta-Harness
references.principle: benchmark harnesses should preserve traces, budgets, and holdout-style controls while optimizing only against verifiable evidence
